final_comments: You didn't address the real reason I published preprints, which is because a co-author made me do it, or the real reason I cited preprints, because a reviewer made me do it. Otherwise, I generally don't follow the preprint literature unless I have to. In several cases, preprints have been used maliciously against me, in that groups that don't normally publish them posted preprints while my manuscripts were under review. Of course, I cannot prove these PIs were the reviewers, but it is quite likely they saw my submission and posted their preprint to prevent getting scooped.

final_comments: The peer reviewed journal system is totally broken. Folks are waiting up to a year for a decision in many of the so called important journals.  And the page charges are through the roof - over $5K for some journals.  I am involved in several large multi-Pi programs and we are all WAITING for things to get published so we can link our results together. The only way forward seems to be preprints. I do not look at them randomly and focus on work that my close colleagues are doing.
